The investment case

Three reasons this building works

01

Structural demand

Income from institutions, not tourists

95%+

Four major universities within 1 km. Seven embassies in the neighbourhood. Academic and diplomatic tenants sign year-long leases. No seasonal vacancy, no platform dependency.

02

Zero CAPEX

Turn-key delivery. Nothing more to spend.

€0

Delivered finished to energy class A standard — PV panels, zone HVAC, 10 cm insulation. Your total investment equals the purchase price. Ready to rent from completion.

€250k

The same Greek residency permit available on island properties priced at €800,000. Central Athens. 350 m from the metro. 15 minutes' walk from the Acropolis.

The district

Vacancy below city average. Structural reasons why.

in Athens

The Exarcheia district is defined by long-term tenants — students, academics, and diplomatic staff — who sign year-long leases and rarely leave. Vacancy sits well below the Athens average. That is not a forecast. It is the observable pattern of this neighbourhood over decades.

Gross rental yield~3% on purchase price
Payback period~33 years
Ownership structureFreehold — new development

How yield scales

Yield is 3% of the unit's purchase price. A €300,000 unit generates €9,000/year gross. A €350,000 unit generates €10,500/year. The figure scales linearly with the unit price.

Note on figures

~3% gross yield is an expected, not guaranteed figure. No rental management is included. Net yield after property tax, maintenance, and management fees will be lower. Full financial modelling available on request.

Technical specification

Turn-key delivery, energy class A

A Energy
class
☀
Photovoltaic panels
Building-integrated solar generation. Contributes to energy class A and reduces tenant operating costs.
❄
HVAC with zone control
Full heating and cooling with independent zone control per unit. Cooling SEER > 6.1 — highest efficiency band.
⬜
High-efficiency glazing
U-value < 1.1 W/m²K. Aluminium frames with thermal break. Significantly below standard building code.
⬛
10 cm thermal insulation
Full envelope insulation. Reduces heat loss in winter and cooling load in summer. Low utility costs for tenants.
🚘
Parking included
Dedicated space with every upper-floor unit (levels A–D). Ground floor garden units without parking.
🔑
Turn-key delivery
Flooring, kitchen, bathroom fittings completed. No CAPEX required before rental. Furniture at buyer's discretion.
Epirus AssetsVesta III · Athens

Golden Visa

Greek residency through property ownership

01
Purchase from €250,000
Vesta III qualifies under the Zone B threshold — the lowest qualifying price point for central Athens. Confirm current zone classification before proceeding.
02
Submit the GV application
Filed with the Greek Migration Ministry after completion. Processing typically 3–6 months. A local lawyer is required — Epirus Assets can refer one.
03
Receive 5-year residency permit
Renewable indefinitely while the property is held. Covers the buyer, spouse, and dependent children under 21.
04
Schengen travel access
Visa-free travel across 26 Schengen countries. No minimum stay in Greece required to maintain the permit.

Zone note: The €250,000 threshold applies to Zone B. Zone A — most Aegean islands, southern Athens suburbs, Thessaloniki — now requires €800,000. Exarcheia currently falls in Zone B, but zone boundaries are reviewed periodically. Verify current status before proceeding.
